flashcards.back: A linear data structure that follows the Last In First Out (LIFO) principle. Elements are added and removed from the same end called the 'top'.
A linear data structure that follows the First In First Out (FIFO) principle. Elements are added at the rear and removed from the front.

flashcards.front: What are the basic operations of a Stack?
flashcards.back: Push (add to top), Pop (remove from top), Peek/Top (view top element), isEmpty (check if empty), isFull (check if full).

flashcards.front: What are the basic operations of a Queue?
flashcards.back: Enqueue (add to rear), Dequeue (remove from front), Front (view first element), isEmpty (check if empty), isFull (check if full).
A condition that occurs when trying to push an element onto a stack that has already reached its maximum capacity.
A condition that occurs when trying to pop an element from a stack that is already empty.
Stack of plates, undo/redo operations, browser back button, function call stack, expression evaluation.
People waiting in a line, printer job queue, CPU task scheduling, ticket counter, BFS traversal.

flashcards.front: How do stacks and queues differ?
flashcards.back: Stack: LIFO, single-end access (top). Queue: FIFO, two-end access (front for removal, rear for insertion).

flashcards.front: What is the time complexity of push and pop in a stack?
flashcards.back: Both push and pop operations have O(1) time complexity as they only operate on the top element.
